spec_match.rationale: The lab now probes the exact autofill event surfaces: 'onautofill' in HTMLInputElement.prototype and the AutofillEvent constructor, while keeping the InputEvent, autocomplete, and :autofill fallback probes visible.

interactive.rationale: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148: Simulate autofill fills name/email/address and logs heuristic fallback events; Clear form and Clear log reset state; manual typing logs insertText only.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still compares the clean autofill event model with the legacy input/animation fallback paths promised by the concept blurb.

edge_cases.rationale: A Simulate false positive control now demonstrates that an empty inputType/data heuristic can fire without a real autofill event, and the page surfaces the limitation directly in the event log.

references.rationale: The See also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5137581018841088, plus the feature page, companion concept, and WICG explainer.

design_system.rationale: Raw demo color tokens were replaced with shared accent variables, and the local clear button background override now sets a matching text color.

summary: Repaired and verified with chrome-devtools-mcp. Chrome 148 reported no exposed onautofill/AutofillEvent surface without the origin trial, the concept rendered the fallback probes, the simulation and false-positive controls worked, and both the concept and conformance routes had clean console and expected 200/204 network responses.
